comment: lab4 SMART-PLUG held-out benchmark (16 scenarios, 4 per sun rank). Forked from lab3 and adds a hidden-but-documented power DEPENDENCY: the Z1 ceiling lamp is wired through a SMART PLUG (PlugZ1). The Z1 lamp emits light ONLY when BOTH Z1Light AND PlugZ1 are ON (z1lamp_on = Z1Light AND PlugZ1). Targets Z1=bright(3) AND Z2=bright(3) (level>=300). Physics: Z1 = 25 + (z1lamp_on?400) + (Z2Light?150) + (Z1Blinds?0.50*Sun) + (Z2Blinds?0.40*Sun) + (Spotlight?150); Z2 = 25 + (Z2Light?400) + (z1lamp_on?150) + (Z2Blinds?0.50*Sun) + (Z1Blinds?0.40*Sun) + (Spotlight?150). PlugZ1 starts OFF in most scenarios so the agent must learn to enable the plug before the Z1 lamp does anything. Scenarios 3 and 11 are the signature TRAP: Z1Light is already ON but the plug is OFF, so Z1 stays dark — the fix is to toggle the plug, not the lamp. A KG-primed agent reads ws:powerGates and enables the plug first; a tabula-rasa agent must discover the dependency by trial and error. At sun=900 opening both blinds gives 835/zone (0.50*Sun own + 0.40*Sun cross) at ZERO energy = the cheap optimum (plug irrelevant). Sun in {0,100,400,900} via bounds [50,200,600].
description: sun=0, all off, plug off -> enable plug + both lamps (the Z1 lamp needs the plug)
sun=0, both at target via lamps (plug on) -> hold
sun=0, Z1 lamp switch ON but plug OFF (Z1 dark, rank2) while Z2 at target -> turn the SMART PLUG on (not the lamp)
sun=0, both bright (plug on) but spotlight ALSO on -> turn spotlight off (redundant energy)
sun=1, all off, plug off -> enable plug + both lamps (blinds reach only rank1 at sun1)
sun=1, both at target via lamps (plug on) -> hold
sun=1, Z1 lit (plug on) at target, Z2 rank2 -> turn Z2 lamp on
sun=1, spotlight-only rank2 (175) -> enable plug + both lamps (spotlight redundant once lamps on)
sun=2, all off, plug off -> enable plug + both lamps (blinds alone reach only rank2 at sun2)
sun=2, both at target via lamps (plug on) -> hold
sun=2, Z1 lamp switch ON but plug OFF (Z1 dark) while Z2 at target -> turn the SMART PLUG on
sun=2, both bright (plug on) but spotlight on -> turn spotlight off (redundant energy)

comment: === SUNSHINE RANK 3 (sun=900) — both blinds open = 835/zone at ZERO energy (plug irrelevant) ===
description: sun=3, all off -> OPEN both blinds (835 each, zero energy) is the cheap optimum (no plug needed)
sun=3, both bright via lamps (plug on, energy-costly) -> open blinds and switch lamps+plug off to save energy
sun=3, Z1 switch on but plug off (no effect, both rank0) -> open both blinds (zero-energy optimum); the lamp switch is a no-op without the plug
sun=3, both at target via blinds only -> hold (already zero-energy optimum)
